pattern: Arrival time is monotone and non-negative, so a Dijkstra-style relaxation where each edge contributes a wait-then-cross delay finds the earliest reachable time at every node.
hints: Track the earliest time you can be standing at each node; waiting is free, so arriving earlier is never worse.
To use edge (u, v, s, e) from node u at time t, you must depart at some integer in [max(t, s), e]; the best is to leave as early as possible.
Run Dijkstra keyed on earliest arrival time: from time t at u, edge (u, v, s, e) yields arrival max(t, s) + 1 provided max(t, s) <= e.

edge_inputs.description: two routes, the slower-start one wins
no outgoing edge from the source
edge window closes before you can arrive
must wait at the start before the only edge opens

stress.description: random small DAG-ish graphs with assorted time windows, checked against a relaxation oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = []
    m = rng.randrange(0, n * 2)
    for _ in range(m):
        u = rng.randrange(0, n)
        v = rng.randrange(0, n)
        if u == v:
            continue
        s = rng.randrange(0, 20)
        e = s + rng.randrange(0, 20)
        edges.append([u, v, s, e])
    return [n, edges]
